mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing output that supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases correlate with negative or flat 20-day S&P 500 returns during mid-cycle expansions, yielding a Rank IC below -0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 3.5% triggers immediate market repricing for aggressive central bank tightening, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 3.5% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and declining unemployment rates confirm robust labor market tightening that sustains positive equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product directly translate to higher corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth shows no predictive power for 20-day S&P 500 returns, with a cumulative Rank IC below 0.01 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals immediate supply chain constraints that trigger negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days during high-inflation regimes.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO falls below the 0.5% monthly threshold.
